sections.subtitle: Team Form Analysis
sections.text: Tottenham Hotspur are struggling, with only 2 wins in their last 5 matches and a current 17th place in the league. Brighton & Hove Albion, in contrast, are in fine form, unbeaten in their last 4 matches and sitting 8th with strong attacking output.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters, both teams have scored, with Brighton winning the most recent match 3-2 at home. Matches between these sides tend to be high-scoring, with over 2.5 goals in 4 of the last 5 head-to-heads.

sections.subtitle: Key Player Impact
sections.text: Tottenham are missing key players like Heung-min Son and James Maddison, which impacts their attack. Brighton’s Kaoru Mitoma and Yankuba Minteh are in good form, likely to exploit Tottenham’s defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Brighton have a better defensive record and conversion rate compared to Tottenham, who have underperformed their xG and conceded frequently. Tottenham’s high corner count per game (6.5) could be a factor, but their poor league position reflects defensive frailties.

sections.subtitle: Match Context
sections.text: With Tottenham at home but in poor form, and Brighton showing consistency, the away side might have the edge. Expect a competitive game with goals on both sides given recent trends.
